data.title: FaceTec appoints Cameron D'Ambrosi as Head of Strategic Partnerships for NA and EU. Global leader in 3D Face Verification software.
data.description: FaceTec, a prominent provider of 3D Face Verification software worldwide, recently appointed Cameron D'Ambrosi as the Head of Strategic Partnerships for North America and Europe. The announcement was made on January 6, 2026, in Summerlin, Nevada. Cameron D'Ambrosi will oversee partnerships for FaceTec in these key regions, bringing his expertise to drive growth and collaboration.

data.title: AirJoule Technologies (NASDAQ: AIRJ) plans public offering of Class A common stock, no set size or terms yet.
data.description: Ronan, Montana, saw AirJoule Technologies Corporation (NASDAQ: AIRJ) announcing the start of an underwritten public offering of its Class A common stock, valued at $0.0001 per share. This move was made under an effective shelf registration statement on Form S-3 (File No. 333-291527) previously submitted to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). However, the offering's completion is reliant on market conditions, with uncertainties about the final size or terms of the offering.

data.title: SF transpo company offers free 30-day employee shuttle trial, aims to boost retention, lower parking costs.
data.description: A prominent transportation company in the Bay Area, Bauer's Intelligent Transportation, is providing businesses with an opportunity to try luxury employee shuttle services at no cost and without any obligation. These services aim to enhance retention rates, lower parking expenses, and promote sustainability. The initiative was announced in San Francisco on January 13, 2026. Bauer's Intelligent Transportation has over 37 years of experience in the industry.

data.title: Warrior Met Coal awarded federal coal leases in Alabama by BLM, expanding operations.Signed by Secretary Doug Burgum.
data.description: Brookwood, Alabama - Warrior Met Coal, Inc. (NYSE: HCC) is pleased to announce that its subsidiaries, Warrior Met Coal Mining, LLC and Warrior Met Coal BC, LLC, have secured federal coal leases from the Bureau of Land Management. This will allow for the growth of their mining activities in Alabama. The leases were obtained through BLM's competitive Lease By Application (LBA) process and were officially signed by Secretary Doug Burgum. The successful completion of this process signifies a significant step in expanding Warrior Met Coal's operations in the region.

data.title: Keenova presents new Dupuytren's Contracture survey at AASH meeting. Important survey results discussed. #KeenovaPresentation #DupuytrensSurvey
data.description: Keenova Therapeutics plc will present new findings on Dupuytren's contracture (DC) and XIAFLEX® (collagenase clostridium histolyticum, or CCH) at the American Association for Hand Surgery Meeting. The analysis, to be shared in Dublin on Jan. 13, 2026, highlights the effectiveness of XIAFLEX® in treating DC. This development underscores the growing significance of this treatment, with an increasing number of patients experiencing positive outcomes. The study is set to shed light on the promising impact of XIAFLEX® in addressing DC, indicating a substantial advancement in hand surgery research.

data.title: Mercantile Bank and FHLBank Indianapolis award $2.2 million in grants to West Michigan nonprofits for affordable housing.
data.description: A recent partnership between Mercantile Bank and the Federal Home Loan Bank of Indianapolis aims to broaden affordable housing choices and boost investments in neighborhoods and the local economy of Grand Rapids, Michigan. The collaboration, announced on Jan 13, 2026, seeks to address housing affordability issues by leveraging resources and support. This initiative is anticipated to make a significant impact by helping a larger number of individuals find affordable housing solutions and by driving economic growth in the area.

data.title: EMCO Industries introduces high-performance mono-leaf spring for U.S. trailer OEMs, with increased domestic production at new facility.
data.description: An Oklahoma-based manufacturer, EMCO Industries, announced plans to enhance its on-time delivery and boost domestic production at its state-of-the-art facility amidst uncertain times. The company, recognized for producing leaf springs for trailers, is headquartered in Tulsa. The initiative is set to increase their manufacturing capacity and bolster efficiency. The developments are expected to position the manufacturer strongly in the market. The upgrade is part of their strategy to navigate challenging conditions while maintaining quality.
